behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model’s diarization/label output quality, not just local wiring. A mock STT response would hide real mislabeling failures (MOCK-HIDE). Real API/system effects exist, but Agentic is higher because correctness depends on AI output content (LLM-DEP rule applied to speech model output). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model-output property (speaker labels), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real recorded multi-speaker meeting through the actual STT API and assert speaker-labeled transcript segments map to known attendees with sufficient attribution correctness for downstream summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic inside local code (MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module failure boundary in description). No real OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model judgment (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require module handoff to fail; can fail within parser logic alone.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignment phrases, assert parser returns normalized assignment objects with correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real LLM includes/omits in generated summary content (LLM-DEP yes). Stubbed LLM would always pass and hide omission/coverage failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: behavior text validates one summary-generation outcome constraint, not a required multi-agentic chained pipeline test.
behaviors.test_description: Send a transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization LLM and assert output action_items cover every attendee with at least one assigned task.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is about deterministic routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is local transformation logic. No real infra or model output dependency is required. Rejected Integration: no explicit cross-module state-handoff failure is required by this behavior wording.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision entities and narrative fields, assert mapper places decisions only in `decisions` schema nodes and not in generic narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests model extraction/placement quality in real generated output, which is model-judgment dependent (LLM-DEP yes). Mocked outputs would hide real misses or misplacement (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: single output-quality check for one summarization step, not multiple agentic stages requiring sequential correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM summarization on transcripts with known decisions and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section with acceptable recall and not only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential processing steps (split -> per-segment process -> merge) and includes agentic segment summarization/transcription decisions across steps (MULTI-STEP yes). Real failures often emerge from cross-step context loss and merge coherence; single-step agentic or integration tests can miss these. Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires sequence orchestration across multiple agentic outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production chunking/orchestration, process each chunk with real STT/LLM path sequentially, then assert merged final summary is structurally valid and preserves cross-segment continuity (actions/decisions not dropped or duplicated).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real upload, transcription, summarization, and delivery; it spans real infra and multiple agentic steps in sequence (REAL-INFRA yes, MULTI-STEP yes). Lower levels cannot catch cross-stage automation/handoff failures. Rejected System/Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires full chained pipeline completion without manual intervention.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run with a real meeting audio file from ingestion to output delivery, asserting unattended completion, valid structured schema, and no stage requires manual retry/repair.
